es.text: No tener pelos en la lengua
es.literal: to have no hairs on the tongue
es.meaning: To speak bluntly; to say exactly what one thinks.
es.example: Mi abuela no tiene pelos en la lengua, te dice lo que piensa.

it.literal: to have no hairs on the tongue
it.example: Non ha peli sulla lingua: dice sempre la verità.
it.exampleTranslation: She doesn't mince words: she always tells the truth.

bridge.note: A near-identical pair — a genuine gift. pelos/peli, lengua/lingua. Only the preposition shifts: en la vs. sulla.

interference.es_to_it: The preposition shifts: en la lengua becomes sulla lingua.
interference.it_to_es: The preposition shifts back: sulla lingua becomes en la lengua.

review.notes: Strong candidate for an early 'bridge' showcase entry. Cue drafted by Claude 2026-07-20 from the entry's meaning and bridge — English situational framing, not a language claim, so it needs editorial taste rather than native review. Override freely.

hook.headline: Neither language has hairs on its tongue. Only the preposition moves.
hook.subhead: pelos/peli, lengua/lingua — and then en la becomes sulla.

cue: How do you describe someone who says exactly what they think?
